# Cover Letter — UW Facilities Business Intelligence Developer

---

Five years in UW Finance and Operations gives me a specific kind of context for this role: I know what it means to produce reporting for a UW audience, to work within UW's administrative systems, and to care about the difference between a dataset that is technically correct and one that a department head can actually use.

The technical side I have built from the outside. My current work includes production ETL pipelines (Apache Airflow, Python), operational dashboards in Flask backed by normalized SQLite schemas, and multi-source data integration across REST APIs and structured data formats. The tools I have worked with are not Tableau and Power BI; they are Python and SQL, with Flask as the reporting layer. The gap is real. The underlying skills — query design, data modeling, translating a business question into a structured output — transfer directly.

The work I find most interesting in this JD is the internal consultant piece: sitting with a business partner, understanding what question they are actually asking, and building something they can use rather than something technically impressive. I led a SmartConnect/Great Plains ERP integration in my last contract role by starting with that question. I have been doing the same thing across data science, financial operations, and research systems for most of my career.

UW Facilities is a large, operationally complex organization that generates exactly the kind of messy, multi-system data that rewards careful modeling and honest documentation. That sounds like the right kind of problem.

Simon-Hans Edasi
